<findings>

[observation] [warning] 9 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
[observation] [warning] High coupling (0.67) — 35 imports target "prd-operations-core"
[observation] [warning] Low cohesion (0.33) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
[observation] [warning] Cohesion 0.33 and coupling 0.67 indicate this zone is over-broad; extracting MCP server wiring (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts) into its own zone would isolate transport concerns from presentation logic.
[observation] [warning] health.ts appears in both entry points and as a core module (core/health.ts), suggesting the CLI and domain health logic may not be cleanly separated.
[observation] [info] status-sections.ts and token-format.ts are pure rendering helpers that carry no domain logic — they're stable leaf nodes and their presence in a high-coupling zone is incidental, not structural.
[suggestion] [info] Zone "rex-status-mcp-cli" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
[suggestion] [info] Zone name 'rex-status-mcp-cli' is the only zone name in the package that concatenates three concern labels; rename to a single-concern name (e.g. 'mcp-presentation-layer') after splitting MCP wiring from CLI rendering, to align with the single-concern naming convention used by all other zones
[suggestion] [warning] mcp-tools.ts imports 16 core modules by path (confirmed by import count) — mcp.ts + mcp-tools.ts together form a missing use-case layer; extracting them to src/mcp/ would collocate the transport boundary files, make the missing application layer explicit, and reduce the zone's file-count to coherent CLI-only members
[suggestion] [warning] Zone "Rex Status & MCP CLI" (rex-status-mcp-cli) has critical risk (score: 0.67, cohesion: 0.33, coupling: 0.67) — requires refactoring before new feature development
[pattern] [warning] src/cli/commands/constants.ts is a shared-constants file accidentally parked inside a high-level CLI/MCP zone; its 7-zone import fan-out means it is effectively a foundation-layer file masquerading as a CLI file — move it to src/schema/ or src/core/ to reflect its actual scope
[relationship] [warning] The single bidirectional import with task-selection-engine (1+1) almost certainly flows through constants.ts rather than representing genuine domain coupling; verify and sever if so to achieve true leaf-node status for task-selection-engine
[anti-pattern] [warning] cli/index.ts dispatchAdd() (lines 113-169) implements multi-mode command routing (manual/smart/file) using file-extension detection, flag inspection, and dynamic imports inside the CLI entry point. Business dispatch logic belongs in a dedicated router or command-factory module, not in the CLI bootstrapper.
[anti-pattern] [warning] mcp-tools.ts directly imports 18 core module paths (tree, stats, delete, next-task, transitions, timestamps, parent-completion, dag, cascade-reset, move, merge, verify, reorganize, reorganize-executor, health, facets) with no application-layer intermediary. This is a missing use-case layer: the MCP transport handler is coupled to 18 internal implementation details rather than a stable operation interface. Any internal core refactor propagates directly to the MCP surface.

</findings>

<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.33) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.67) — 35 imports target "prd-operations-core"
- 9 entry points — wide API surface, consider consolidating exports
- Low cohesion (0.33) reflects this zone blending rendering helpers (status-sections, token-format), infrastructure (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts), and domain queries (health, stats) without a unifying abstraction
- The MCP tooling and CLI display layers have very different change cadences and concerns — separating them would raise cohesion
- Coupling at 0.67 is high, driven by heavy imports from both core and unit-cli zones, making this zone a hub rather than a leaf
- Cohesion 0.33 and coupling 0.67 indicate this zone is over-broad; extracting MCP server wiring (mcp.ts, mcp-tools.ts) into its own zone would isolate transport concerns from presentation logic.
- status-sections.ts and token-format.ts are pure rendering helpers that carry no domain logic — they're stable leaf nodes and their presence in a high-coupling zone is incidental, not structural.
- health.ts appears in both entry points and as a core module (core/health.ts), suggesting the CLI and domain health logic may not be cleanly separated.
- Zone "rex-status-mcp-cli" has files across 8 directories — consider consolidating under a dedicated directory
- constants.ts anchored in this zone is the root cause of 7-zone fan-out coupling — every zone that needs shared CLI constants acquires an implicit dependency on the MCP/status zone, artificially inflating coupling scores across the entire package
- src/cli/commands/constants.ts is a shared-constants file accidentally parked inside a high-level CLI/MCP zone; its 7-zone import fan-out means it is effectively a foundation-layer file masquerading as a CLI file — move it to src/schema/ or src/core/ to reflect its actual scope
- The single bidirectional import with task-selection-engine (1+1) almost certainly flows through constants.ts rather than representing genuine domain coupling; verify and sever if so to achieve true leaf-node status for task-selection-engine
- mcp-tools.ts imports 18 individual core modules by path (tree, stats, delete, next-task, transitions, timestamps, parent-completion, dag, cascade-reset, move, merge, verify, reorganize, reorganize-executor, health, facets) — the MCP presentation layer is directly coupled to 18 internal implementation modules with no application/use-case layer intermediary; renaming any core module requires a corresponding update in mcp-tools.ts
- cli/index.ts concentrates complex multi-mode dispatch logic for the add command (lines 113-169: dispatchAdd handles manual mode, smart mode, and file mode via extension detection and flag inspection) inside the CLI entry point rather than a dedicated router module — this puts mode-routing business logic in the wrong layer
- mcp.ts imports all 15 MCP tool handlers from mcp-tools.ts as a flat list — the handler registry is implicit (15 named imports) rather than a structured map, meaning adding a new MCP tool requires edits in three places: mcp-tools.ts, mcp.ts import list, and the server registration block
- mcp-tools.ts directly imports 18 core module paths (tree, stats, delete, next-task, transitions, timestamps, parent-completion, dag, cascade-reset, move, merge, verify, reorganize, reorganize-executor, health, facets) with no application-layer intermediary. This is a missing use-case layer: the MCP transport handler is coupled to 18 internal implementation details rather than a stable operation interface. Any internal core refactor propagates directly to the MCP surface.
- cli/index.ts dispatchAdd() (lines 113-169) implements multi-mode command routing (manual/smart/file) using file-extension detection, flag inspection, and dynamic imports inside the CLI entry point. Business dispatch logic belongs in a dedicated router or command-factory module, not in the CLI bootstrapper.
- Zone name 'rex-status-mcp-cli' encodes three concerns (status rendering, MCP wiring, CLI dispatch) unlike every other zone name in the package which encodes a single concern — the name itself is a cohesion warning signal that the zone needs splitting
- status-sections.ts and token-format.ts are only consumed by sibling files within src/cli/commands/ (status.ts, analyze.ts, usage.ts) — they are co-located rendering helpers whose low fan-out makes them candidates for a src/cli/renderers/ subdirectory, visually separating rendering leaves from command entry points
- report.ts lives in data-persistence-validation while status.ts, token-format.ts, and status-sections.ts live here — both are CLI display concerns, yet they are zone-separated; the zone boundary for display logic is drawn inconsistently across these two zones
- Zone name 'rex-status-mcp-cli' is the only zone name in the package that concatenates three concern labels; rename to a single-concern name (e.g. 'mcp-presentation-layer') after splitting MCP wiring from CLI rendering, to align with the single-concern naming convention used by all other zones
- mcp-tools.ts imports 16 core modules by path (confirmed by import count) — mcp.ts + mcp-tools.ts together form a missing use-case layer; extracting them to src/mcp/ would collocate the transport boundary files, make the missing application layer explicit, and reduce the zone's file-count to coherent CLI-only members
- [call graph] 646 internal calls, 142 outgoing, 59 incoming (cohesion: 0.82, coupling: 0.18)

</insights>
